refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall continuously monitor its operational status and report availability metrics against the customer-defined target of ≥ 0.97, ensuring that any deviation triggers appropriate alerts and actions to maintain or restore service.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is a high-level KPI. This refinement breaks it down into actionable components: monitoring, reporting, target comparison, and reactive alerting/action. This makes it more testable and allocable.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Simulate various failure scenarios (e.g., component failure, network outage) and verify that the system correctly detects the status change, calculates the impact on availability, reports the deviation, and triggers an alert within specified timeframes. Compare reported availability against actual operational uptime.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-004.1: The NGHE solution shall monitor its operational status and the status of its constituent subsystems and components.
FR-NGHE-004.2: The NGHE solution shall calculate and report operational availability metrics based on monitored status data.
FR-NGHE-004.3: The NGHE solution shall compare calculated availability metrics against the customer-defined target.
FR-NGHE-004.4: The NGHE solution shall generate alerts and initiate predefined actions when availability falls below the target or is projected to do so.

candidate_dps: Implement a centralized health monitoring and reporting service that aggregates status from all subsystems, calculates availability, and provides a dashboard/API for reporting.
Distribute availability calculation and reporting to individual subsystems, with a master aggregator for overall KPI.
Utilize a third-party availability monitoring platform integrated with NGHE components.
selected_dp.selected_dp: Implement a centralized health monitoring and reporting service that aggregates status from all subsystems, calculates availability, and provides a dashboard/API for reporting. This service will also be responsible for comparing against targets and triggering alerts.

coupling_check.notes: This DP centralizes the availability logic, minimizing coupling by providing a single source of truth for availability calculations and reporting. Subsystems only need to provide status updates, not perform complex availability logic.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: All critical subsystems and components can expose their operational status in a standardized, machine-readable format.
Assumption: The centralized service has sufficient processing power and reliability to handle continuous status updates and calculations.
Risk: Latency in status reporting could lead to delayed availability metric updates, impacting real-time decision-making.
Risk: A single point of failure for the centralized service could prevent any availability monitoring.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The overarching system responsible for integrating and managing all sub-components to deliver the complete NGHE functionality.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives operational status data from various NGHE components (e.g., Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) for availability calculation.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Publishes calculated availability metrics and alerts to external stakeholders (e.g., Site Manager dashboard, Maintenance/Quality systems).

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Triggers safety-related alerts if availability degradation impacts safety-critical functions.

module_containment_scores.notes: This module is the most appropriate for housing the overall availability monitoring and reporting logic, as it spans the entire system and aggregates data from all parts. It is the 'system of systems' integrator.
The Vehicle module would only contain availability for the vehicle itself, not the entire NGHE solution. This would lead to high diffusion of the availability logic across multiple modules.
The Teleoperation module would only contain availability for teleoperation components, not the entire NGHE solution. High diffusion.

While safety is critical, the Safety Services module is focused on safety functions, not overall system availability. It might contribute status, but not own the overall KPI calculation. Some diffusion if it were to own this.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The 'NGHE Solution' module is the natural owner for the overall availability KPI monitoring and reporting, as it represents the top-level system integration and is responsible for the end-to-end solution's performance. This centralizes the logic and minimizes diffusion.
containment_risks: Risk: The 'NGHE Solution' module might become a monolithic 'god object' if too many cross-cutting concerns are assigned to it without proper internal decomposition.
Risk: Performance bottlenecks if the 'NGHE Solution' module is overwhelmed by status updates from numerous sub-components.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP directly addresses the refined FR by proposing a centralized mechanism for monitoring, calculating, reporting, and alerting on availability. It provides a clear 'how' for the 'what'.

diffusion_risks: The risk of the 'NGHE Solution' module becoming a 'god object' if internal decomposition is not carefully managed at the next level. This is a design risk, not a diffusion risk for this level's assignment.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The DP is well-contained within the 'NGHE Solution' module, as it is the logical place for system-level KPIs. This avoids diffusing availability logic across multiple lower-level modules.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of the refined FR (monitoring, reporting, alerting) is feasible by testing the 'NGHE Solution' module's ability to correctly process telemetry, calculate availability, and generate alerts. Interfaces are clear.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the overall availability responsibility to the 'NGHE Solution' module is clear and logical, aligning with its role as the system integrator.

decision.justification: The current level of decomposition has successfully refined the FR, selected a suitable DP, and assigned it to an appropriate IM. However, the refined FR is not atomic and requires further decomposition into its sub-FRs to define the detailed 'how' for monitoring, calculation, and alerting.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-004.1: The NGHE solution shall monitor its operational status and the status of its constituent subsystems and components.
FR-NGHE-004.2: The NGHE solution shall calculate and report operational availability metrics based on monitored status data.
FR-NGHE-004.3: The NGHE solution shall compare calculated availability metrics against the customer-defined target.
FR-NGHE-004.4: The NGHE solution shall generate alerts and initiate predefined actions when availability falls below the target or is projected to do so.

assumptions: All critical subsystems and components can expose their operational status in a standardized, machine-readable format.
The centralized service has sufficient processing power and reliability to handle continuous status updates and calculations.
open_risks: Latency in status reporting could lead to delayed availability metric updates, impacting real-time decision-making.
A single point of failure for the centralized service could prevent any availability monitoring.
The 'NGHE Solution' module might become a monolithic 'god object' if too many cross-cutting concerns are assigned to it without proper internal decomposition.
evidence_needed: Definition of standardized status reporting format for all NGHE components.
Performance requirements and benchmarks for the centralized availability service.
Reliability and redundancy strategy for the centralized availability service.
